articles.title: Cricket SA in hot water over foreign currency kept in HQ safe for years | News24
articles.description: Cricket South Africa kept US dollars and other foreign currencies at its Johannesburg headquarters for years, potentially violating exchange control regulations, according to an explosive resignation letter by board member Muditambi Ravele seen by News24.

more.title: Congo files ICJ case against Rwanda over role in conflict
more.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo has filed a case against Rwanda at the International Court of Justice over its role in the long-running conflict in the east, the government said on Friday. In a statement, Congo accused Rwanda of breaching international conventions, including those on the prevention of genocide, racial discrimination and torture. Congo said Rwanda […]

more.title: Lesotho seeks compensation and economic support from South Africa
more.description: Lesotho’s foreign affairs minister Limpho Tau. Photo: Sechaba Mokhethi. Lesotho’s foreign affairs minister says South Africa has a moral obligation to help address poverty and the lack of economic growth in Lesotho. Minister Limpho Tau told the South African government that land dispossession, migrant labour and apartheid-era military raids had contributed to Lesotho’s economic hardship. [...]

more.title: Tazmin Brits insists Proteas Women will not be distracted by focusing on net run rate against Bangladesh
more.description: Opening batter says South Africa will focus solely on beating Bangladesh after her unbeaten century against the Netherlands kept the Proteas in the Women's T20 World Cup semi-final race, with their fate also hinging on Australia's clash with India.

more.title: Siyafana Sonke Urges Government Accountability and Protection of Migrants’ Rights in South Africa
more.description: PRETORIA, Gauteng — A coalition of 130 civil society organizations operating under the banner of Siyafana Sonke has called for urgent government accountability and the protection of migrants’ rights in South Africa. The advocacy group warns that widespread public anger regarding unemployment and collapsing public services is being dangerously misdirected at vulnerable foreign nationals, allowing [...]

more.title: Bafana Bafana’s World Cup Heroics Must Spark a South African Football Renaissance
more.description: The South African national football team have defied the logic of the global spreadsheets and analysts and all soccer pundits combined by doing the unthinkable and probably the biggest upset of the 2026 FIFA World Cup to date.
